root_text.an4.45:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an4.45:1.2: Atha kho rohitasso devaputto abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho rohitasso devaputto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.45:2.1: “Yattha nu kho, bhante, na jāyati na jīyati na mīyati na cavati na upapajjati, sakkā nu kho so, bhante, gamanena lokassa anto ñātuṁ vā daṭṭhuṁ vā pāpuṇituṁ vā”ti?
root_text.an4.45:2.2: “Yattha kho, āvuso, na jāyati na jīyati na mīyati na cavati na upapajjati, nāhaṁ taṁ gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ daṭṭheyyaṁ patteyyanti vadāmī”ti.
root_text.an4.45:3.3: ‘yattha kho, āvuso, na jāyati na jīyati na mīyati na cavati na upapajjati, nāhaṁ taṁ gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ daṭṭheyyaṁ patteyyanti vadāmī’”ti.
root_text.an4.45:4.1: “Bhūtapubbāhaṁ, bhante, rohitasso nāma isi ahosiṁ bhojaputto iddhimā vehāsaṅgamo.
root_text.an4.45:4.2: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, evarūpo javo ahosi, seyyathāpi nāma daḷhadhammā dhanuggaho sikkhito katahattho katūpāsano lahukena asanena appakasirena tiriyaṁ tālacchāyaṁ atipāteyya.
root_text.an4.45:4.3: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, evarūpo padavītihāro ahosi, seyyathāpi nāma puratthimā samuddā pacchimo samuddo.
root_text.an4.45:4.4: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, evarūpena javena samannāgatassa evarūpena ca padavītihārena evarūpaṁ icchāgataṁ uppajji:
root_text.an4.45:4.6: So kho ahaṁ, bhante, aññatreva asitapītakhāyitasāyitā aññatra uccārapassāvakammā aññatra niddākilamathapaṭivinodanā vassasatāyuko vassasatajīvī vassasataṁ gantvā appatvāva lokassa antaṁ antarāyeva kālaṅkato.
root_text.an4.45:5.3: ‘yattha kho, āvuso, na jāyati na jīyati na mīyati na cavati na upapajjati, nāhaṁ taṁ gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ daṭṭheyyaṁ patteyyanti vadāmī’”ti.
root_text.an4.45:6.1: “‘Yattha kho, āvuso, na jāyati na jīyati na mīyati na cavati na upapajjati, nāhaṁ taṁ gamanena lokassa antaṁ ñāteyyaṁ daṭṭheyyaṁ patteyyan’ti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.45:6.2: Na cāhaṁ, āvuso, appatvāva lokassa antaṁ dukkhassa antakiriyaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an4.45:6.3: Api cāhaṁ, āvuso, imasmiṁyeva byāmamatte kaḷevare sasaññimhi samanake lokañca paññāpemi lokasamudayañca lokanirodhañca lokanirodhagāminiñca paṭipadanti.

translation_text.an4.45:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an4.45:1.2: Then, late at night, the glorious godling Rohitassa, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an4.45:2.1: “Sir, is it possible to know or see or reach the end of the world by traveling to a place where there’s no being born, growing old, dying, passing away, or being reborn?”
translation_text.an4.45:2.2: “Reverend, I say it’s not possible to know or see or reach the end of the world by traveling to a place where there’s no being born, growing old, dying, passing away, or being reborn.”
translation_text.an4.45:3.2: how well said this was by the Buddha.
translation_text.an4.45:4.1: Once upon a time, I was a seer called Rohitassa, son of the benefactors. I was a sky-walker with psychic powers.
translation_text.an4.45:4.2: I was as fast as a light arrow easily shot across the shadow of a palm tree by a well-trained expert archer with a strong bow.
translation_text.an4.45:4.3: My stride was such that it could span from the eastern ocean to the western ocean.
translation_text.an4.45:4.4: Having such speed and stride, this wish came to me:
translation_text.an4.45:4.5: ‘I will reach the end of the world by traveling.’
translation_text.an4.45:4.6: I traveled for my whole lifespan of a hundred years—pausing only to eat and drink, go to the toilet, and sleep to dispel weariness—and I passed away along the way, never reaching the end of the world.
translation_text.an4.45:5.2: how well said this was by the Buddha.”
translation_text.an4.45:6.1: “Reverend, I say it’s not possible to know or see or reach the end of the world by traveling to a place where there’s no being born, growing old, dying, passing away, or being reborn.
translation_text.an4.45:6.2: But I also say there’s no making an end of suffering without reaching the end of the world.
translation_text.an4.45:6.3: For it is in this fathom-long carcass with its perception and mind that I describe the world, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.
translation_text.an4.45:7.1: The end of the world can never
translation_text.an4.45:7.3: But without reaching the end of the world,
translation_text.an4.45:8.1: So an intelligent person, understanding the world,
translation_text.an4.45:8.2: has completed the spiritual journey, <j>and gone to the end of the world.
translation_text.an4.45:8.3: Assuaged, knowing the end of the world,
translation_text.an4.45:8.4: they do not hope for this world or the next.”
